methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Cellcolabs, a Series A biotech & health company founded by Mattias Bernow, has raised $25M+ to date. Valuation assessment relies on a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including later-stage companies like Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B) and Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B, Seed), as well as public benchmarks including RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression, anchoring to the $25M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a post-money valuation of $50M. Peer-Median analysis across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied owing to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target company.

The triangulated valuation range is $42.5M (low) to $931M (high), with a median estimate of $430M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material stage and maturity variance within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the actual magnitude of Cellcolabs' current progress toward Series B readiness, clinical or commercial inflection points relative to named peers, and the specificity of its technology differentiation within the sector.
